description: Literature terms without accepted matches in their preferred ontologies.

gaps.proposed_definition: A environmental variable corresponding to 'carbon-cycle functioning'.

occurrences.title: Growing season carbon gas exchange from peatlands used as a source of vegetation donor material for restoration
occurrences.evidence_text: it is possible that these sites will return to their original carbon-cycle functioning after disturbance

gaps.proposed_definition: A environmental variable corresponding to 'CO2 flux'.

occurrences.title: Growing season carbon gas exchange from peatlands used as a source of vegetation donor material for restoration
occurrences.evidence_text: Higher sedge cover resulted in more negative NEE values (greater CO2 uptake) across the fen

gaps.proposed_definition: A environmental variable corresponding to 'exotic grasses'.

occurrences.title: Long‐term effects of prairie restoration on plant community structure and native population dynamics
occurrences.evidence_text: treatments that included grass-specific herbicide were the most effective at reducing the abundance of exotic grasses

gaps.proposed_definition: A environmental variable corresponding to 'Methane emissions'.

candidate_matches.label: methane gas emission process
candidate_matches.definition: Methane is emitted during the production and transport of coal, natural gas, and oil. Methane emissions also result from livestock and other agricultural practices and by the decay of organic waste in municipal solid waste landfills.

candidate_matches.label: cold seep
candidate_matches.definition:
MERGED DEFINITION:
TARGET DEFINITION: A cold seep is a seep in which methane and other hydrocarbon-rich fluids rise to the sea floor.
--------------------
SOURCE DEFINITION: An area of the ocean floor where hydrogen sulfide, methane and other hydrocarbon-rich fluid seepage occurs. Cold seeps are distinct from hydrothermal vents: the former's emissions are of the same temperature as the surrounding seawater, whereas the latter's emissions are super-heated.

occurrences.title: Growing season carbon gas exchange from peatlands used as a source of vegetation donor material for restoration
occurrences.evidence_text: Methane emissions were higher from donor sites compared to natural sites due to the high water table

gaps.proposed_definition: A environmental variable corresponding to 'moss cover'.

candidate_matches.label: area of moss-dominated vegetation
candidate_matches.definition: According to the NLCD 2011, at least 80% of these areas are covered by mosses.  Such thresholds - which are themselves fuzzy - are not strictly asserted in this definition. The original definition notes that this is intended as an "Alaska only" category.

candidate_matches.label: tundra ecosystem
candidate_matches.definition: Tundra ecosysems are often underlaid by permafrost, with the result that drainage is bad and the soil may be saturated for long periods. It does not have a permanent snow-ice cover.  Productivity is low in this biome because of the extremes of climate.

candidate_matches.label: cloud forest
candidate_matches.definition: A montane forest ecosystem which 1) is evergreen, 2) present on mountains where there is frequent cloud and mist cover, 3) which has a large proportion of ferns and mosses in its plant communities, growing on all tree trunks and branches present in the ecosystem, and 3) harvests aerosolised water and adds it to the terrestrial water supply.

occurrences.title: Growing season carbon gas exchange from peatlands used as a source of vegetation donor material for restoration
occurrences.evidence_text: GEPmax became more negative (greater CO2 uptake) in plots with higher moss cover across the bog sites

gaps.proposed_definition: A environmental variable corresponding to 'native species abundance'.

occurrences.title: Long‐term effects of prairie restoration on plant community structure and native population dynamics
occurrences.evidence_text: addition of native seed effectively increased both the richness and abundance (defined as percent cover) of native species

gaps.proposed_definition: A environmental variable corresponding to 'native species richness'.

occurrences.title: Long‐term effects of prairie restoration on plant community structure and native population dynamics
occurrences.evidence_text: plots that received supplemental seeding continued to exhibit higher richness of native species than those left unseeded

gaps.proposed_definition: A environmental variable corresponding to 'population spread'.

occurrences.title: Long‐term effects of prairie restoration on plant community structure and native population dynamics
occurrences.evidence_text: there is evidence that all eight species colonized new plots since the last round of monitoring

gaps.proposed_definition: A environmental variable corresponding to 'sedge cover'.

candidate_matches.label: area of sedge- and forb-dominated herbaceous vegetation
candidate_matches.definition: According to the NLCD 2011, at least 80% of these areas are covered by sedge or forbs.  Such thresholds - which are themselves fuzzy - are not strictly asserted in this definition. The NLCD description also notes that land covered by sedge tundra and sedge tussock tundra are subsumed in this category. The original definition notes that this is intended as an "Alaska only" category.

candidate_matches.label: tundra ecosystem
candidate_matches.definition: Tundra ecosysems are often underlaid by permafrost, with the result that drainage is bad and the soil may be saturated for long periods. It does not have a permanent snow-ice cover.  Productivity is low in this biome because of the extremes of climate.

candidate_matches.label: area of dwarf scrub
candidate_matches.definition: According to the NLCD 2011, areas of dwarf scrub are dominated by shrubs less than 20 centimeters tall with shrub canopy typically greater than 20% of total vegetation. This type is often co-associated with grasses, sedges, herbs, and non-vascular vegetation. The NLCD 2011 notes that this is intended as an Alaska only land cover. Such thresholds - which are themselves fuzzy - are not asserted in this definition.

candidate_matches.label: tundra mire
candidate_matches.definition: Peatlands with a shallow peat layer, only about 500 mm thick, dominated by sedges and grasses. They form in permafrost areas, covering around 110,000 to 160,000 km2 in Alaska, Canada, and the former USSR.

occurrences.title: Growing season carbon gas exchange from peatlands used as a source of vegetation donor material for restoration
occurrences.evidence_text: Higher sedge cover resulted in more negative NEE values (greater CO2 uptake) across the fen

gaps.proposed_definition: A environmental variable corresponding to 'total vegetation cover'.

candidate_matches.label: area of evergreen forest
candidate_matches.definition: According to the NLCD 2011, areas of evergreen forest are dominated by trees generally greater than 5 meters tall, and making up more than 20% of total vegetation cover. The NLCD 2011 continues to state that more than 75% of these trees should maintain their foliage despite seasonal change - the canopy is never without green foliage. Such thresholds - which are themselves fuzzy - are not asserted in this definition.

candidate_matches.label: area of dwarf scrub
candidate_matches.definition: According to the NLCD 2011, areas of dwarf scrub are dominated by shrubs less than 20 centimeters tall with shrub canopy typically greater than 20% of total vegetation. This type is often co-associated with grasses, sedges, herbs, and non-vascular vegetation. The NLCD 2011 notes that this is intended as an Alaska only land cover. Such thresholds - which are themselves fuzzy - are not asserted in this definition.

candidate_matches.label: area of scrub
candidate_matches.definition: According to the NLCD 2011, areas of scrub or shrub are dominated by shrubs; less than 5 meters tall with shrub canopy typically greater than 20% of total vegetation. This class includes true shrubs, young trees in an early successional stage or trees stunted from environmental conditions. Such thresholds - which are themselves fuzzy - are not asserted in this definition.

candidate_matches.label: area of mixed forest
candidate_matches.definition: According to the NLCD 2011, areas of mixed forest are dominated by trees generally greater than 5 meters tall, and making up more than 20% of total vegetation cover. The NLCD 2011 continues to state that neither deciduous or evergreen trees make up more than 75% of the total tree cover. Such thresholds - which are themselves fuzzy - are not asserted in this definition.

candidate_matches.label: area of deciduous forest
candidate_matches.definition: According to the NLCD 2011, areas of deciduous forest are dominated by trees generally greater than 5 meters tall, and making up more than 20% of total vegetation cover. The NLCD 2011 continues to state that more than 75% of these trees should shed foliage simultaneously in response to seasonal change. Such thresholds - which are themselves fuzzy - are not asserted in this definition.

occurrences.title: Growing season carbon gas exchange from peatlands used as a source of vegetation donor material for restoration
occurrences.evidence_text: At both the bog and fen GEP max became more negative with increasing total plant cover

gaps.proposed_definition: A environmental variable corresponding to 'tree recruitment'.

occurrences.title: Growing season carbon gas exchange from peatlands used as a source of vegetation donor material for restoration
occurrences.evidence_text: tree recruitment at the donor sites was limited

gaps.proposed_definition: A environmental variable corresponding to 'vegetation re-establishment'.

occurrences.title: Growing season carbon gas exchange from peatlands used as a source of vegetation donor material for restoration
occurrences.evidence_text: vegetation re-establishment on donor sites was rapid

gaps.proposed_definition: A management intervention corresponding to 'burn treatments'.

occurrences.title: Long‐term effects of prairie restoration on plant community structure and native population dynamics
occurrences.evidence_text: three perennial forb species (Eriophyllum lanatum, Achillea millefolium, and Ranunculus occidentalis) responded more positively to both of the burn treatments compared with other treatments

gaps.proposed_definition: A management intervention corresponding to 'disturbance treatments'.

occurrences.title: Long‐term effects of prairie restoration on plant community structure and native population dynamics
occurrences.evidence_text: both seeding and disturbance treatments could positively influence native species abundance over the long term

gaps.proposed_definition: A management intervention corresponding to 'Donor material collection'.

occurrences.title: Growing season carbon gas exchange from peatlands used as a source of vegetation donor material for restoration
occurrences.evidence_text: Donor material collection involves collecting the top 5–10 cm of the living vegetation from an appropriate donor peatland.

gaps.proposed_definition: A management intervention corresponding to 'grass-specific herbicide'.

occurrences.title: Long‐term effects of prairie restoration on plant community structure and native population dynamics
occurrences.evidence_text: treatments that included grass-specific herbicide were the most effective at reducing the abundance of exotic grasses

gaps.proposed_definition: A management intervention corresponding to 'supplemental seeding'.

occurrences.title: Long‐term effects of prairie restoration on plant community structure and native population dynamics
occurrences.evidence_text: plots that received supplemental seeding continued to exhibit higher richness of native species than those left unseeded

gaps.proposed_definition: A variable corresponding to 'abundance'.
gaps.candidate_parent: ELMO environmental variable or PATO quality

candidate_matches.label: cartilaginous
candidate_matches.definition: Cartilage is a type of dense connective tissue. It is composed of specialized cells called chondrocytes (CL:0000138) that produce a large amount of extracellular matrix composed of collagen fibers, abundant ground substance rich in proteoglycan, and elastin fibers.

occurrences.title: Long‐term effects of prairie restoration on plant community structure and native population dynamics
occurrences.evidence_text: treatments that included grass-specific herbicide were the most effective at reducing the abundance of exotic grasses

gaps.proposed_definition: A variable corresponding to 'CH4 flux'.
gaps.candidate_parent: ELMO environmental variable or PATO quality

occurrences.title: Growing season carbon gas exchange from peatlands used as a source of vegetation donor material for restoration
occurrences.evidence_text: Methane emissions were higher from donor sites compared to natural sites due to the high water table

gaps.proposed_definition: A variable corresponding to 'cover'.
gaps.candidate_parent: ELMO environmental variable or PATO quality

candidate_matches.label: setose
candidate_matches.definition: Setose as a label is used extensively to describe insect phenotypes that have hairy-like appearances (i.e., they are covered in setae, not the hairs of mammals). A bristle is a type of seta.

candidate_matches.label: pubescent hair
candidate_matches.definition: A pilosity quality of being covered with short hairs or soft down.

candidate_matches.label: wet
candidate_matches.definition: A wetness quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being covered by a liquid.

candidate_matches.label: dry
candidate_matches.definition: A wetness quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's not being covered by a liquid.

candidate_matches.label: scaly
candidate_matches.definition: A texture quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being covered or partially covered with scales.

candidate_matches.label: hairy
candidate_matches.definition: A pilosity quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being covered with hair or hairlike projections.

candidate_matches.label: warty
candidate_matches.definition: A texture quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being covered with warts or projections that resemble warts resulting in a hard rough surface.

candidate_matches.label: wetness
candidate_matches.definition: A quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of whether the bearer's being covered by a liquid.

occurrences.evidence_text: three perennial forb species (Eriophyllum lanatum, Achillea millefolium, and Ranunculus occidentalis) responded more positively to both of the burn treatments compared with other treatments

candidate_matches.label: subulate
candidate_matches.definition: A shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being linear, very narrow, tapering to a very fine point from a narrow base.

candidate_matches.definition: Needs redefined or obsoleted. Is this the same as genetic incompatibility? Moved to organismal quality.
